title: Chemical investigation of the roots of Xanthium strumarium
author: Sankar Kumar Maitra
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Medicinal Chemistry Division, Indian Institute of Chemical Biology, Jadavpur, Kolkata-700 032, India Bethune College Research Laboratory, Kolkata-700 006, India 

  Manuscript received 15 October 2004, revised 16 November 2005, accepted 21 February 2006  

 Chemical investigation of the methanolic extract of the roots of Xantllium strumarium (compositae) has resulted in the isolation of stigmasteroi-3-  \(O\)  -β-D-glucopyranoside (1), 2-methylanthraquinone (2) and stigmasterol. Although stigmasterol was reported earlier from the aerial part of his plant, but there was no report of isolation of I and 2 from this plant source. This is the first report of isolation of 1 and 2 from this plant with detailed spectral and chemical studies. 
        Compound I though reported earlier to be present in minute quantity in a fraction containing a mixture of sterols but there was no report of its detailed spectral and chemical studies. Compound 2, even though a known compound, has not been isolated from this plant before.
